assertions.text: V1 was traveling westbound in lane two of a five-lane undivided roadway (two westbound lanes, center turn lane, two eastbound lanes).
V1 traversed the lane lines and entered lane one (the adjacent westbound lane) before departing the left side of the roadway.
After departing the left side of the roadway, V1 initiated a clockwise yaw (rotation).
V1's left side impacted an overhead traffic signal pole.
The impact with the pole caused V1 to rotate rapidly clockwise around the pole.
V1 struck the overhead traffic signal pole and then its front section struck a concrete electric box—at least two distinct impacts occurred.
During the event, V1 split into two pieces: a rear box and a front section.
The rear box came to rest in the center of the roadway.
The front section continued westerly after splitting and impacted a concrete electric box with its right side plane.
After impacting the concrete electric box, the front section came to rest facing easterly.

Sequence: V1 traversed lane lines into lane one, departed left side of roadway, initiated clockwise yaw, left-side impact with traffic signal pole, rotated around pole and split, rear came to rest in roadway center while front continued west and impacted concrete electrical box, then front came to rest facing east.
The vehicle split into two pieces (indicating a severe collision).
The driver was ejected from V1.

verdicts.evidence: Roadway geometry and lane counts are not present in telemetry; vehicle speed values are available but lane assignment cannot be determined from sensors alone.
Lane position changes (traversed lane lines into adjacent lane and departed roadway) are not observable in the provided telemetry channels (no GPS/path/lateral position).

Gyro/rotation axes are not provided, but steering and crash-pulse show rotation consistent with clockwise yaw: steering peak signed dev [ -98.7 ](T2) at [ -0.9 s ](T2) indicates a large steering input, and the cumulative lateral delta-V reached [4.3 km/h](T2) by [0.21 s](T2) while longitudinal delta-V reached [7.3 km/h](T2) by [0.24 s](T2), consistent with a yawing motion after leaving roadway.

Impact direction (left-side impact with an overhead traffic signal pole) is not directly observable: device-frame axes are undocumented and there is no object-type/contact-side sensor in the telemetry.

Crash-pulse shows cumulative lateral delta-V of [4.3 km/h](T2) by [0.21 s](T2) and longitudinal delta-V of [7.3 km/h](T2) by [0.24 s](T2), indicating rapid rotation/impact dynamics consistent with rotation about a pole after impact.
Telemetry shows a crash pulse but cannot identify separate external impacts or their targets; number of distinct impacts with external objects is not determinable from available channels.
Vehicle breakup (splitting into two pieces) is not recorded in the telemetry channels; no sensor provides structural integrity/failure events.
Final resting positions (rear box in center of roadway) are not observable in the telemetry; GPS/position trace is not available.
Telemetry does not identify subsequent object impacts (concrete electric box) or the side of impact; device-frame axes are undocumented.
Final vehicle orientation (front section resting facing easterly) is not available in the provided telemetry.

The full sequence involves lane changes, collisions, object impacts and resting positions which cannot be fully reconstructed from the available channels; only timing and delta-V magnitudes are available.

EDR summary shows significant crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V of [7.3 km/h](T2) and lateral delta-V of [4.3 km/h](T2) and the vehicle metadata indicates a pickup truck; such delta-V magnitudes are consistent with a severe collision that could cause structural breakup.
Occupant ejection is not recorded in the vehicle telemetry channels; belt code indicates driver belt status [1](T1) but does not directly confirm ejection.

result.rationale: Telemetry provides crash pulse magnitudes ([7.3 km/h longitudinal](T2), [4.3 km/h lateral](T2)), steering inputs ([ -98.7 deg peak](T2)), and EDR summary ([max_delta_v_long_kmh 10.85](T1)) that support a substantial impact and rotation, but lacks GPS/position, object/contact-side, and structural-failure sensors needed to verify lane-level maneuvers, impact sides, multiple impacts, breakup, resting positions, and ejection.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse
